events.title: South Sudan risks return to full-blown civil war as violence escalates
events.description: At least 169 killed in raid near Sudan border as clashes between government and opposition forces intensify South Sudan is reeling from an escalating conflict between the government-aligned army and opposition forces and allied groups that observers say risks returning the country to a full-blown civil war. Violent confrontations in the world’s youngest country between the military, which is loyal to President Salva Kiir, and insurgents believed to be allied to the suspended vice-president, Riek Machar, have increased in recent weeks. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 South Sudan risks return to full-blown civil war as violence escalates

events.title: ‘Imperialist undertones’: global south condemns US-Israeli war with Iran
events.description: China calls it unacceptable to ‘kill leader of sovereign state’, while South Africa questions ‘pre-emptive’ justification Middle East crisis – live updates The US-Israeli war on Iran has been condemned as illegal across much of the global south, with China saying it was unacceptable to “blatantly kill the leader of a sovereign state”. Many countries objected that negotiations between the US and Iran over its nuclear programme and missile capability were not given a chance to succeed before Washington and Israel began bombing, and analysts often saw the war in terms of a colonial-style exercise of might. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Imperialist undertones’: global south condemns US-Israeli war with Iran

events.title: Austin bar shooting victims named as FBI investigates potential terrorism nexus
events.description: Austin shooting victims Savitha Shan, 21, Jorge Pederson, 30, and Ryder Harrington, 19, were killed in a suspected terrorist attack at Buford's Backyard Beer Garden on Sunday morning.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Austin bar shooting victims named as FBI investigates potential terrorism nexus

events.title: Jordanian arrested for visa offences after alleged Bondi attackers visited his coffee shop during Philippines stay
events.description: Military used in arrest of Mohammad Odeh Saleh, owner of business allegedly visited by the Akrams before Australia’s deadliest terror attack Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Jordanian national whose coffee shop was visited by the alleged gunmen behind December’s Bondi beach terrorist attack – and who frequently traveled to Australia and the Middle East – has been arrested in the Philippines for overstaying his visa. Authorities investigating the gunmen’s month-long stay in Mindanao Island, in the country’s south, alleged that Mohammad Odeh Saleh, 65, had travelled to Australia in the years before his arrest in Pagadian City on Monday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Jordanian arrested for visa offences after alleged Bondi attackers visited his coffee shop during Philippines stay

events.title: At least 20 killed as cash-laden military cargo plane crashes in Bolivia
events.description: Riot police use teargas to disperse people gathering around wreckage of plane loaded with money from central bank At least 20 people have died and dozens have been injured after a military cargo plane carrying banknotes crashed while landing near Bolivia’s capital on Friday, damaging about a dozen vehicles on a highway and scattering bills on the ground, an official has said. Footage from local media showed people rushing to collect banknotes while police in riot gear tried to disperse them using teargas. Authorities were later seen setting the money alight in a bonfire at the scene of the crash. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ At least 20 killed as cash-laden military cargo plane crashes in Bolivia

events.title: Iran crisis: Schoolgirls killed, thousands displaced and aid compromised
events.description: On the fourth day of Israeli and United States airstrikes against Iran and amid growing violence and instability in the Middle East, the UN urgently called for protection of civilians and warned of growing displacement and humanitarian needs.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran crisis: Schoolgirls killed, thousands displaced and aid compromised

events.title: Iran crisis: Nuclear watchdog urges restraint amid ongoing strikes
events.description: As the violent escalation in the Middle East entered a third day on Monday sparked by the bombing of Iran by Israel and the United States, the head of the UN atomic energy agency urged a return to diplomacy, given the “increasing risk to nuclear safety” in the region.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran crisis: Nuclear watchdog urges restraint amid ongoing strikes

events.title: Did the US‑Israel strikes on Iran break international law?
events.description: From disputed self‑defense claims to the killing of Iran’s leader, legal experts say the US‑Israeli attack defies UN rules. Iran’s response and mixed global reactions highlight fears that international law is eroding.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Did the US‑Israel strikes on Iran break international law?

events.title: Major parties knock back inquiry into racism and sexism in the Senate – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Australia sends crisis teams to Middle East as thousands of travellers and expats remain stranded Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Farrell says $15bn of trade could be impacted by war Labor’s trade minister, Don Farrell , says a “relatively small” amount of Australia’s exports go through the Middle East as the war escalates in the region. A relatively small amount, about $15bn worth of trade goes through the Middle East. Obviously, that’s very important for those companies that are trading there. Our trade is, in fact, increasing in the Middle East. We now have a free trade agreement with the United Arab Emirates. Already, our beef trade has doubled in the six months that that trade agreement has been in operation. But of course, all of that gets affected by this uncertainty of the war in the Middle East. The legal basis of these strikes is ultimately a matter for the United States and Iran, sorry, and Israel, is ultimately a matter for the United States and Israel. We know Iran has failed to comply with UN security council resolutions on its nuclear program. We know what Iran has been doing over many years. I think it is important for us to remember this has not started with these strikes. This has been going on for decades, including in Australia. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Major parties knock back inquiry into racism and sexism in the Senate – as it happened

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Strikes continue from US, Israel and Iran as UN urges restraint
events.description: Violent escalation in the Middle East has entered a third day as coordinated US and Israeli strikes against Iran aimed at regime change continue to cause loss of life and damage across the region, prompting Iranian missile and drone counter-strikes hitting targets in multiple countries. Explosions, airspace closures and military alerts have been reported from Tehran to the Gulf states and beyond. Follow our live coverage with updates from across the United Nations system. UN News app users can follow here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Strikes continue from US, Israel and Iran as UN urges restraint

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Conflict continues across region amid US, Israeli and Iranian strikes
events.description: Violence in the Middle East is continuing into a fifth day, with US and Israeli strikes against Iran and Iranian missile and drone attacks reported across several countries in the region. The escalating confrontation is disrupting airspace, transport and daily life while raising fears of a wider regional conflict. Follow our live coverage for real-time updates, reactions and humanitarian developments from across the United Nations system. UN News app users can follow the coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Conflict continues across region amid US, Israeli and Iranian strikes

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE: UN Security Council meeting in emergency session over Iran
events.description: The UN Security Council met in emergency session in the aftermath of major airstrikes across Iran on Saturday by the United States and Israel. In retaliation, Tehran launched its own attacks throughout the Middle East. UN chief António Guterres told ambassadors the action risks “igniting a chain of events that nobody can control in the most volatile region of the world.” He added that “everything must be done to prevent further escalation.” Follow our live coverage from UN Web TV below. UN News app users can follow here and full meetings coverage can be found daily, here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE: UN Security Council meeting in emergency session over Iran

events.title: Broadest evacuation order for Lebanon issued by Israeli army
events.description: The Israeli military ordered the ​evacuation of ‌a swathe of southern Lebanon on March 4th, asking residents to move north. This evacuation is the broadest evacuation order yet in this new conflict. Meanwhile, Syria has closed its border with Lebanon after a warning it would be targeted by Israe. FRANCE 24's Renee Davis has more from Beirut.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Broadest evacuation order for Lebanon issued by Israeli army

events.title: Article 2(4): The UN Charter's prohibition on using force
events.description: The recent US-Israel attack on Iran spotlights Article 2(4) of the UN charter again, which was drafted after World War II to ban unprovoked threats or use of force amongst countries.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Article 2(4): The UN Charter's prohibition on using force

events.title: Iraq, caught in the middle, braces for Iran war impact
events.description: Iraq is the only country in the Middle East to be targeted by both sides in the current conflict. There, the war could impact everything from the next government to intercommunal violence and civil servants' salaries.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iraq, caught in the middle, braces for Iran war impact

events.title: Iranian drone strikes shut down Qatar LNG production facilities, as energy prices surge

events.title: US and Ecuador forces launch operation to fight drug trafficking
events.description: Washington says the two countries are combatting what the US calls designated terrorist organisations in the South American country.
events.ticker_label: 💥 US and Ecuador forces launch operation to fight drug trafficking

events.title: US military launches operation in Ecuador to combat drug trafficking
events.description: US Southern Command said joint mission with Ecuadorian forces involves ‘decisive action’ against narco-terrorists US and Ecuadorian forces have launched joint operations to combat drug trafficking , the US Southern Command said on Tuesday, but neither side gave more details. Southern Command, which encompasses 31 countries through South and Central America and the Caribbean, said in a statement on X that the “decisive action” was aimed at combating illicit drug trafficking. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 US military launches operation in Ecuador to combat drug trafficking

events.title: A global superpower with a quiet voice: China 'not as loud or influential' beyond the Global South
events.description: As China’s annual “Two Sessions” gathers thousands of political delegates in Beijing, Oliver Farry is pleased to welcome Jean-François di Meglio, Honorary Chair of Asia Centre. Beyond the 5 percent growth target, China is undergoing a broader structural transition: demographic decline, slower consumption, renewed tensions with the US and the long-term effects of the real estate crisis. As conflict rages in the Middle East, Chinese leaders seek to balance domestic stability with an unpredictable and rapidly shifting global landscape.

events.title: Deadly US submarine strike sinks Iranian warship off Sri Lanka's coast
events.description: S‌ome 148 sailors are still missing after a US ⁠submarine ‌strike sank ⁠an Iranian warship ​in ‌the Indian Ocean, Sri Lankan authorities said Wednesday. The authorities said they had managed to rescue 32 people on board the IRIS Dena.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Deadly US submarine strike sinks Iranian warship off Sri Lanka's coast

events.title: Energy prices start climbing after war closes Strait of Hormuz down
events.description: Since the start of US Israeli strikes on Iran Saturday, the Middle East has been gripped by widespread conflict and chaos, disrupting important shipping routes for oil and other goods. In an attempt to curb rising prices, US president Donald Trump says the US will provide political risk insurance for vessels passing through the strait of Hormuz. Traffic through the strait has been blocked for days as companies fear Iranian attacks on shipping vessels.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Energy prices start climbing after war closes Strait of Hormuz down

events.title: Cuba charges six exiles with terrorism in wake of deadly speedboat attack
events.description: Detainees accused of coming from the US with intent to sow chaos and attack military units on Communist-ruled island Cuban prosecutors have formally charged six people with crimes of terrorism after a US-flagged speedboat was involved in a deadly shootout with Cuba’s coast guard last week. The US-based Cuban defendants are accused of packing a boat with weapons and heading toward Cuba in hopes of destabilising the government in Havana. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Cuba charges six exiles with terrorism in wake of deadly speedboat attack

events.title: Iran war: Why is Russia not coming to Tehran's aid?
events.description: The beleaguered Iranian regime, with very few international allies, has been counting on Moscow's support amid the ongoing US-Israeli strikes, but so far it has been left deeply disappointed.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Why is Russia not coming to Tehran's aid?

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Melania Trump presides over historic session on children in conflict
events.description: The UN Security Council convened in New York under extraordinary circumstances Monday: amid escalating conflict in the Middle East and repeated calls from the UN for restraint by all sides, the Council turns its attention to a long-standing but under-addressed crisis facing children in war zones. The United States, which holds the rotating presidency for March, decided to have First Lady Melania Trump chair the meeting. Usually, the UN ambassador or senior official takes the gavel. While there have been instances of First Ladies taking part in Security Council meetings, today was the first time a sitting First Lady has had the gavel. Follow speaker by speaker below, and UN News app users can go here. For daily in-depth meetings coverage, go here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Melania Trump presides over historic session on children in conflict

events.title: Attacks on Iran and retaliatory strikes ‘undermine international peace and security’

events.title: US plans for Iran remain unclear as it defends "pre-emptive" strikes
events.description: On the fourth day of US-Israeli bombardment of Iran, US officials gave more reasons for their offensive in the country, though their plan for after the war remains unclear. Stated US motivations have also shifted over time, from intervening on the behalf anti-regime protesters in January to "preemptively' striking the country.

events.title: US assets in Gulf states targeted by Iranian strikes
events.description: A fire started in the United States embassy in the Saudi capital of Riyadh after it was hit by two drones suspected to be from Iran. The Saudi Ministry of Defence says the strike on the embassy early on March 3rd caused “minor material damage” to the compound. It comes as the region sees more attacks targeting US assets amid Iran’s retaliation to US-Israeli strikes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US assets in Gulf states targeted by Iranian strikes

events.title: Unilateral US strikes on Iran show "power consolidation around the presidential authority"
events.description: In a press conference in Washington, several Democrat lawmakers, all of whom had previously served in the army, denounced what they called the Trump's administration's "war of choice" in Iran. This took place ahead of debates ahead of a vote of a war powers resolution, which gives lawmakers an opportunity to demand congressional approval before any further attacks are carried out.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Unilateral US strikes on Iran show "power consolidation around the presidential authority"

events.title: 'The needs are immense': Aid groups scramble to help displaced in Lebanon
events.description: One of the officials leading efforts to provide humanitarian aid for people who have been forced to flee their homes in Lebanon has told FRANCE 24 that the needs are immense. Many of those displaced are from the capital Beirut, amid the Israeli bombings there. Others are from the south of the country, where Israeli forces are carrying out what they call a limited incursion as they tell civilians to leave their homes. In Perspective, we spoke to Adéa Guillot from CARE France.
events.ticker_label: 💥 'The needs are immense': Aid groups scramble to help displaced in Lebanon

events.title: ‘Adventurism has had its day’: speedboat shootout leaves Miami’s exiled Cubans bewildered
events.description: Few clues as to how 10 heavily armed men intercepted on stolen speedboat came together from across Florida or what they hoped to achieve Foot traffic was slow outside the Bay of Pigs Museum on Calle Ocho in Miami’s Little Havana neighbourhood. A few tourists in T-shirts and shorts bypassed the gallery dedicated to one of the most fateful days in Cuba’s history and headed instead to nearby Máximo Gómez Park to take photographs of Cuban exiles playing dominoes. This is the street at the heart of the Cuban expat community of more than 1 million people where tens of thousands partied through the night in November 2016 to celebrate the death of Fidel Castro, and where they gathered in sorrow almost exactly 30 years ago to mourn four Cuban-Americans shot down by the communist country’s air force as they conducted a mission for the humanitarian exile group Brothers to the Rescue. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 ‘Adventurism has had its day’: speedboat shootout leaves Miami’s exiled Cubans bewildered

Reports of African men being attracted to Russia by promises of jobs and ending up on Ukraine’s frontlines have become more frequent in recent months, creating tensions between Moscow and some of the countries involved. events.description: Beijing’s foreign ministry urges ‘all parties’ to avoid escalation as number of crossings drops 60% in one day How escalating Iran conflict is driving up oil and gas prices – a visual guide Business live – latest updates The Chinese government has called for vessels passing through the strait of Hormuz to be protected by all sides in the escalating Iran conflict, as shipping freight rates soared. Maritime traffic through the strait – a narrow channel on Iran’s southern border that connects the Persian Gulf with the Gulf of Oman – has effectively been closed since the US and Israel launched missile attacks on Iran at the weekend, prompting a retaliation from Tehran. events.title: Charting the US-Israel war with Iran
events.description: The US-Israeli attacks on Iran have raised tensions in the Middle East. Here's a look at the Iran war in charts and maps.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Charting the US-Israel war with Iran

events.title: Trump, Spain’s Sanchez trade barbs over Iran war
events.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez hit back on Wednesday at US President Donald Trump's criticisms of Madrid's refusal to let US planes use its bases to attack #Iran. #Trump called #Spain a "terrible" ally on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump, Spain’s Sanchez trade barbs over Iran war

events.title: Iran war opens cracks within Trump's MAGA base
events.description: President Trump has launched into a war while he’s facing some of the lowest approval ratings of his political career. A recent poll showed that just one in four Americans approved of his attack on Iran. But it’s not just the general American public: The war is also beginning to show some cracks in support from his die-hard “Make American Great Again” base.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war opens cracks within Trump's MAGA base

events.title: Some travelers stranded in Dubai are paying huge sums for private charter flights out
events.description: Tens of thousands of airline passengers are stranded by the Iran war that has spread across the Gulf region, but some wealthy travelers are getting out — by paying large sums for luxury flights to Europe via airports that are safe from attacks
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Some travelers stranded in Dubai are paying huge sums for private charter flights out

events.title: Pakistan says it killed 67 Afghan security force members in fifth day of fighting
events.description: Pakistan says that Afghan forces have attacked Pakistani military positions along the border, triggering intense clashes that left 67 Afghan troops and one Pakistani soldier dead
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Pakistan says it killed 67 Afghan security force members in fifth day of fighting

events.title: Metal detector scans front yard of Savannah Guthrie’s missing mother as sister to get car back
events.description: Volunteers used a metal detector to search the yard of missing 84-year-old Nancy Guthrie’s home. A $1M+ reward is offered as police continue investigating her Feb. 1 disappearance.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Metal detector scans front yard of Savannah Guthrie’s missing mother as sister to get car back

events.title: Pentagon identifies 4 of 6 US service members killed in drone attack and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Florida man who wrestled cop's gun away and killed him, executed after final appeals rejected
events.description: Florida man Billy Leon Kearse, 53, was executed Tuesday after prosecutors say he fatally shot police officer Danny Parrish in 1991 traffic stop.

events.title: Iran nuclear talks didn't 'pass the smell test' before Trump launched strikes, says Vance
events.description: Vice President JD Vance said U.S. envoys conducted three rounds of talks with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in Geneva, but negotiations failed.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran nuclear talks didn't 'pass the smell test' before Trump launched strikes, says Vance

events.title: Horses help heal soldiers amid Israel’s mounting PTSD crisis after Oct 7 attacks
events.description: The founder of the Transcending Trauma Project, Dr. Anita Shkedi, wanted to help Israeli veterans heal from PTSD through equine therapy, honoring her late son's memory.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Tricia Tuttle to remain Berlinale head — with new code of conduct
events.description: Facing political backlash following Pro-Palestinian speeches during the festival's closing gala, Tuttle received widespread support from the international film industry.

events.title: Cuba charges 6 with terrorism over speedboat shootout
events.description: Cuba said the speedboat carrying weapons came from the United States to sow chaos. However, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said it was not a US operation.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Cuba charges 6 with terrorism over speedboat shootout

events.title: Guterres slams international failure to manage safe migration
events.description: A 2018 agreement that aims to strengthen international cooperation on migration management must become reality, the UN Secretary-General said on Friday in New York.

events.title: UK stops issuing student visas for 4 countries
events.description: The UK Home Office has announced an "emergency brake" on study visas for nationals from Afghanistan, Cameroon, Myanmar and Sudan. It said people were using the visas to enter as students and subsequently seek asylum.

events.title: First French repatriation flight lands in Paris from the Middle East
events.description: The French foreign minister said several repatriation flights for French ⁠nationals, around 400,000 of whom are in the region, were planned for March 4th. It has deployed consular teams at Israel's borders with Egypt and Jordan to facilitate land exits so people can fly onward, and has put in place a similar mechanism in the UAE at borders with Oman and Saudi Arabia, where airspace has remained open. FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali breaks it down for us.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 First French repatriation flight lands in Paris from the Middle East

events.title: Carney says he backs strikes on Iran 'with some regret' as world order frays
events.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says he supports the strikes on Iran “with some regret” as they represent an extreme example of a rupturing world order
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Carney says he backs strikes on Iran 'with some regret' as world order frays

events.title: Gulf states intercept hundreds of Iranian missiles and drones, issue joint condemnation with US
events.description: Gulf states said they intercepted hundreds of Iranian missiles and drones after U.S. and Israeli strikes against Tehran, as regional tensions surged.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gulf states intercept hundreds of Iranian missiles and drones, issue joint condemnation with US

events.title: First US soldiers to die in Iran conflict are identified

events.title: An outlier for condemning Israel’s Gaza genocide, Spain says no to Iran war

events.title: After Iran’s warning, Europe fails to unite on war launched by US, Israel

events.title: Trump's new reasoning for Iran strikes. And, takeaways from the first 2026 primaries

events.title: Strikes resume on Iran, U.S. military says Tehran's defenses 'severely degraded'

events.title: Iran strikes highlight Dubai influencers' free speech limits

events.title: Iran war: Iranian missile headed towards Turkey destroyed

events.title: Replay: Democrat lawmakers denounce "war of choice" in Iran ahead of Senate vote on war powers

events.title: Dubai influencers 'interrupted' by Iran strikes wake up from a 'senseless dream'
events.description: PRESS REVIEW – Wednesday, March 4: The war in the Middle East has transformed the lives of influencers and expats living in Dubai. But first: papers discuss why US President Donald Trump chose to strike Iran. Also: junior doctors in England use ADHD as an excuse to skip working overnight. Finally, a story about mini (and maxi) pet pigs.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Dubai influencers 'interrupted' by Iran strikes wake up from a 'senseless dream'

events.title: As Iran’s leadership shifts amid war, Hezbollah moves to reset the balance: expert

events.title: UK deploying warship, helicopters to Cyprus after drone strike
events.description: The U.K. deployed warship HMS Dragon to Cyprus after an Iranian drone allegedly struck a British Royal Air Force base. The prime minister vowed to protect 200,000 Britons in the region.

events.title: Ambassador Huckabee describes 'best option' for Americans looking to flee Israel
events.description: U.S. Ambassador Mike Huckabee advises Americans in Israel on evacuation options amid unrest, recommending Egypt route as Ben Gurion Airport remains closed.

events.title: Firebrand anti-American cleric Alireza Arafi seen as contender to replace Iran’s Khamenei
events.description: Iran selects radical Islamist Ayatollah Alireza Arafi to interim leadership council following Khamenei's death, positioning him as potential successor.

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: "Everything is speculation" over Khamenei succession but son set as favourite
events.description: Iran announced that a state funeral for supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was postponed after it was meant to be held on March 4th. This comes as the late supreme leader's successor is set to be appointed, with his son expected to take on the role. But "everything about this nomination is speculation", warns FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 "Everything is speculation" over Khamenei succession but son set as favourite

events.title: Iran has made a 'big mistake', says ex-US spy chief
events.description: Iran has made a "big mistake" by not just attacking US bases and assets in the Gulf states, but also targeting civilians in the region, the former director of the CIA has told Sky News.

events.title: Top Israeli military official reveals operation against Iran involved 'strategic and operational deception'
events.description: Israel announces strategic shift toward regional integration through Abraham Accords as military cooperation with Arab neighbors deepens significantly.

events.title: Gas and oil prices soar and shares tumble on fears conflict could escalate
events.description: Markets react as the conflict in the Middle East intensifies and concerns grow over how long it will last.

events.title: The little town making nearly all of China's lanterns
events.description: A remarkable 80% of the country's lanterns are still made by hand in one small town in Hebei Province.

events.title: Iran’s Women’s Asian Cup team have ‘so much concern’ for families back home
events.description: Players contesting the Women's Asian Cup in Australia share fears for loved ones at home amid US-Israeli attacks.

events.title: Israeli air attack hits Beirut during live report

events.title: Iranian missile hits base housing US troops in Qatar

events.title: Former hostage Louis Arnaud discusses life in Evin prison – and his fears for Iran’s future
events.description: French citizen Louis Arnaud was detained in Iran in 2022 and spent nearly two years in the country’s notorious Evin prison. He tells FRANCE 24 why Israeli-US strikes on Iran have brought new dangers for Iran's prisoners, tens of thousands of whom have been locked up for opposing the regime.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Former hostage Louis Arnaud discusses life in Evin prison – and his fears for Iran’s future

events.title: Violent repeat offender accused in Charlotte knife attack was free despite decade-long rap sheet
events.description: A Charlotte stabbing case reignites debate over repeat offenders after an accused man's extensive criminal history spanning multiple counties comes to light.

events.title: What to know about the 'two sessions',  China's biggest political meeting
events.description: The carefully choreographed annual meetings signal the priorities of the world's second-largest economy.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: A glimpse of Iran, through the eyes of its artists and journalists
events.description: Understanding one of the world's oldest civilizations can't be achieved through a single film or book. But recent works of literature, journalism, music and film by Iranians are a powerful starting point.

events.title: Taiwan indicts 62 linked to alleged scam center in Cambodia
events.description: The Cambodia-based Prince Group is suspected of running multi-billion dollar online fraud and money laundering operations globally.

events.title: Death toll in South African building collapse rises to 9 as city plans demolition

events.title: ‘The world is looking to you for clarity’, UN chief tells AI experts

events.title: Why medicinal plants matter on World Wildlife Day
events.description: Medicinal plants and their varied uses span from ancient medicine to modern cosmetics and pharmaceuticals amid a surging demand for more.

events.title: Justice on the move: Mobile courts offer hope for communities in South Sudan
events.description: Travelling more than 200 kilometres (124 miles) from Yambio, the capital of Western Equatoria State in southwestern South Sudan, a team of justice experts escorted by United Nations peacekeepers moved slowly along rough, dusty roads, determined to reach communities that have waited years for their day in court.

events.title: Fires rage at Iran's Bandar Abbas naval headquarters, Strait of Hormuz traffic stalled
events.description: Bandar Abbas fires rage at Iran's main naval headquarters following possible U.S.-Israeli strikes. President Donald Trump won't rule out sending U.S. troops to Iran.

events.title: US Embassy urges Americans in Iraq to shelter in place until further notice
events.description: The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad told Americans to shelter in place due to violent protests and rising security threats.

events.title: Israel tells people in large parts of southern Lebanon to leave ahead of attacks
events.description: Civilians are being displaced, taking shelter in tents, parks and cars, as Israel retaliates to Hezbollah rocket attack.

events.title: Drone targets military base in Bahrain
events.description: Eyewitness video shows a suspected Iranian drone attack on a military base in Bahrain.

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Liberal senator breaks with party to urge ‘mercy for the children’ of IS-linked Australian women in Syria
events.description: Backbencher Andrew McLachlan describes the 23 children as ‘innocents’ victimised by their parents’ ‘tragic attraction to a horrible ideology’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Liberal senator has called for “mercy” for the 23 Australian children detained in a Syrian camp, as he warned that leaving the group to languish in detention risked making the situation worse for them and the Australian community in the future. After the opposition leader, Angus Taylor , this week suggested the children held in al-Roj camp were “Isis sympathisers”, the backbencher Andrew McLachlan again broke from party lines to appeal for compassion and a resolution to their ongoing plight. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Liberal senator breaks with party to urge ‘mercy for the children’ of IS-linked Australian women in Syria

events.title: U.S. evacuates diplomats from Middle East. And, what to expect from N.C., Texas primaries

events.title: Donald Trump berates Spain, Friedrich Merz listens

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Donald Trump berates Spain, Friedrich Merz listens

events.title: Germany: Fuel prices spike amid Iran conflict
events.description: Prices at the pumps for petroleum and diesel have risen by around one-fifth in a matter of days in Germany. Oil production and shipments have been severely disrupted by the fighting in Iran and the wider Middle East.

events.title: Germany news: Skilled craft sector faces huge labor shortage
events.description: People who work with their hands, from butchers to bakers to candlestick makers, appear to be in short supply. Meanwhile, a court upheld a ruling against a woman who falsely practised as a doctor. DW has the latest.

events.title: Father of accused Georgia school shooter convicted of murder
events.description: The 55-year-old gave his teenage son a rifle for Christmas a year before the deadly rampage. He is believed to be the first parent convicted of murder over a shooting carried out by their child.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Father of accused Georgia school shooter convicted of murder

events.title: US sank Iranian warship, Pentagon announces in press briefing
events.description: A US submarine sank an Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean, Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth said on March 4th. The Pentagon also warned the operation in Iran was "far from over". "We are seeing an administration that is trying to make a very confused narrative seems to take on the air of cohesion", says FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US sank Iranian warship, Pentagon announces in press briefing

events.title: "US lawmakers' efforts to curb Trump's ability to continue war almost sure to fail"
events.description: Lawmakers in the U.S. Senate are set to vote on March 4th on a bipartisan war powers resolution aiming to stop the military campaign against Iran and require that any ​hostilities against it ‌be authorised by Congress. This comes as Trump faces little voter support for the war. But lawmakers' efforts to curb Trump's ability to "goad alone with Israel in this war against Iran are almost sure to fail", warns FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 "US lawmakers' efforts to curb Trump's ability to continue war almost sure to fail"

events.title: Spain's PM is not mincing his words over Trump's war on Iran

events.title: How AI is already reshaping working conditions
events.description: From delivery couriers compelled to follow the demands of online platform algorithms to content moderators who confront pornography and death every day while training artificial intelligence (AI) systems, the impact of new technologies on working conditions is becoming increasingly obvious.

events.title: Mother in affluent Florida community killed 2 children before taking her own life: police
events.description: A Florida mother killed her two children in Lakewood Ranch before taking her own life, authorities determined after a welfare check at a home.

events.title: Israel says fighter jet took down Iranian warplane, the first shootdown of its kind
events.description: Israel’s military said an F-35I “Adir" stealth jet shot down an Iranian Yak-130 over Tehran, the first time the aircraft has destroyed a manned fighter in combat.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel says fighter jet took down Iranian warplane, the first shootdown of its kind

events.title: Two women arrested in Uganda for allegedly kissing in public could face life sentence
events.description: Wendy Faith and Alesi Diana Denise were taken into custody under laws that have outraged LGBTQ+ community and rights activists Two women have been arrested and detained in Uganda after allegedly kissing in public, an act of “same-sex activity” which can lead to a life sentence in the east African country. Wendy Faith, a 22-year-old musician known as Torrero Bae, and Alesi Diana Denise, 21, were taken into custody after police raided their rented room in Uganda’s north-west Arua City last week. Continue reading...

events.title: Is the CIA planning to arm Kurdish forces to spark an uprising in Iran?
events.description: Such a plan could further destabilise Iran rather than promote peace in the country, experts say.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: New Zealand coalition votes to make English an official language as critics decry ‘cynical’ bill
events.description: Push to give English same status as Māori and NZ sign languages triggers backlash from opposition parties and linguistic experts A bill to recognise English as an official language of New Zealand has cleared its first hurdle in parliament amid ridicule from opposition parties and linguists who say it is “unnecessary” and “cynical”. The bill seeks to give English, which is spoken by 95% of the country, the same official status as te reo Māori (Māori language) and New Zealand sign language. The bill said the status and use of the existing official languages would not be affected. Continue reading...

events.title: More generals purged as delegates gather for China’s Two Sessions event
events.description: Spectre of military upheaval will hang over annual meetings where Beijing’s five-year plan will be launched The standing committee of China’s top political advisory body has voted to remove three generals from its ranks as a sweeping purge of the military continues before this week’s annual Two Sessions gathering. The advisory body will meet on Wednesday, while China’s legislature, the National People’s Congress (NPC) – which removed nine generals last week – will start its annual session on Thursday. Collectively the concurrent meetings are referred to as Two Sessions, one of the most important events in China’s political calendar when thousands of delegates arrive in Beijing. Continue reading...

events.title: India news: Millions revel in colors of Holi
events.description: Holi celebrations are in full throttle in India. Meanwhile, airlines in the country are planning to operate dozens of flights to the Middle East.

events.title: Stranded Britons in Middle East - what you need to know as flights back to UK begin
events.description: More than 1,000 British nationals have arrived back in the UK on commercial flights from the Middle East, Sir Keir Starmer has said.

events.title: London police arrest 3 men on suspicion of spying for China
events.description: London police say they have arrested three men suspected of spying for China in violation of the U.K.’s National Security Act

events.title: St. Vincent did not give US authorization for deadly boat strikes, prime minister says

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Minnesota police sergeant, father of two, dies suddenly after brain infection
events.description: Minnesota Police Sergeant Cody Siebert died just 24 hours after brain infection diagnosis, leaving behind two young sons and devastated community.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Minnesota police sergeant, father of two, dies suddenly after brain infection

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Tim Wilson claims he has sold out of his bet against the share market for ‘modest profit’
events.description: Shadow treasurer tells MPs he has cashed in the investment and donated the gains to an advocacy group for gay rights in Iran Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Tim Wilson has sold out of his “terrible” bet against the Australian share market, and claimed to have made a profit from the investment which the shadow treasurer said he would donated to an advocacy group for LGBT rights in Iran. Guardian Australia first reported on the investment , a leveraged product that profits when the benchmark ASX 200 falls, last year. It was viewed as an unusual investment for a politician given the product profits from market slumps, which is linked to the performance of the economy. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump presses NATO partners on support as Hegseth blasts hesitation
events.description: Trump reportedly disappointed in U.K.'s hesitation on Iran campaign as European allies show mixed support for U.S.-Israel military operations against Tehran.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: Rising anger over ‘lop-sided’ and ‘immoral’ US health funding pacts with African countries
events.description: Zimbabwe refuses to sign agreement and Kenya faces a court case over data sharing as new aid deals come under scrutiny A series of bilateral health agreements being negotiated between African countries and the administration of President Donald Trump have been labelled “clearly lop-sided” and “immoral” amid growing outrage at US demands, including countries being forced to share biological resources and data. It emerged this week that Zimbabwe had halted negotiations with the US for $350m (£258m) of health funding, saying the proposals risked undermining its sovereignty and independence. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump suggests US could carry out ‘friendly takeover’ of Cuba
events.description: As tensions between two countries reach new highs, US president says regime is ‘talking with us’ Donald Trump has suggested the US could carry out a “friendly takeover” of Cuba as tensions between Washington and Havana reach a new high after the capture of Venezuela’s Nicolás Maduro. As he left the White House for a campaigning event in Texas on Friday, Trump said: “The Cuban government is talking with us. They’re in a big deal of trouble.” Continue reading...

events.title: China’s Two Sessions: what are the meetings and why do they matter?
events.description: Annual political gathering kicks off this week in Beijing with the economy, technology and the military high on the agenda More generals purged as Chinese delegates gather for event China’s annual Two Sessions meetings begin this week, with thousands of political and community delegates descending on Beijing from across mainland China , Hong Kong and Macau to ratify legislation, personnel changes and the budget over about two weeks of highly choreographed meetings. Continue reading...

events.title: Adelaide University cancels literary festival event with UN Gaza investigator Francesca Albanese
events.description: Festival organisers criticise the university for last-minute booking cancellation of event headlined by special rapporteur for Palestinian territories Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Another free speech row at a literary festival has erupted, with Adelaide University abruptly cancelling a high-profile event featuring UN Special Rapporteur on the occupied Palestinian territories, Francesca Albanese. The move has prompted the festival’s organisers and speakers to accuse the 152 year-old institution of “crumbling in fear”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Adelaide University cancels literary festival event with UN Gaza investigator Francesca Albanese

events.title: Plibersek accuses Mafs of platforming ‘coercive control’ after contestant wanted a woman ‘obedient’ like a dog
events.description: Social services minister Tanya Plibersek criticises hit reality TV show Married at First Sight for ‘messaging which encourages control and dehumanises women’ Sign up for Guardian Australia’s free weekly media newsletter here Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The social services minister, Tanya Plibersek , has accused Australia’s biggest media company, Nine Entertainment, of “normalising” coercive control by airing an exchange in which a Married at First Sight contestant says he wants a woman to be obedient like a dog. Plibersek urged parents not to let their children watch the “dangerous” reality TV juggernaut, which regularly attracts more than 2 million viewers on broadcast television alone. Sign up to get Guardian Australia’s weekly media diary as a free newsletter Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Plibersek accuses Mafs of platforming ‘coercive control’ after contestant wanted a woman ‘obedient’ like a dog
